nativeRange.habitat: Open water above outer reef edges and current-swept drop-offs, where it forms large midwater aggregations feeding on zooplankton

decorPreferences: plenty of live rock with hiding places
open swimming space in front of and above the reef structure

diet.notes: In the wild it feeds primarily on zooplankton in midwater above the reef, supplemented with some algae. In captivity it generally adapts well to frozen and freeze-dried preparations such as vitamin-enriched mysis and brine shrimp, and tends to accept dried marine algae and pellets. Several small feedings per day are commonly advised, especially during acclimation and to discourage occasional nipping at soft corals.

avoidWith: very boisterous tankmates that may intimidate newly introduced specimens

summary: Peaceful planktivorous butterflyfish from the Indian Ocean, with striking black-and-white pyramid markings; generally considered reef-safe and one of the more aquarium-hardy butterflyfishes when given open swimming space.
careNotes: The Zoster Butterflyfish, often traded as the 'Black Pyramid Butterflyfish', is the Indian Ocean sister-species of the better-known Yellow Pyramid Butterflyfish (Hemitaurichthys polylepis) and shares much of its husbandry. It is most easily distinguished by its bold vertical white band across an otherwise dark body, with yellow accents on the dorsal fin tips. In the wild it forms large midwater schools above current-swept outer reef slopes, so in captivity it benefits from open swimming space, brisk flow, and a tank with a long footprint rather than a tall, narrow one. Newly imported specimens are commonly reported to be shy and benefit from plenty of live-rock cover plus several small feedings per day during acclimation. Source temperatures for the Indian Ocean range run warmer than for the Pacific congener: FishBase reports a wild range of about 24.7-29°C, while retail care sheets commonly suggest about 22-26°C; a middle range of roughly 24-27°C is generally advised. Tank-size guidance is also inconsistent across sources (LiveAquaria states about 470 L / 125 US gal as a minimum, while some retailers list as little as 75 US gal, and reef-focused references suggest 500 L or more for a single fish and larger systems for groups), so 500 L is used here as a cautious minimum consistent with the species' active midwater swimming behavior. Generally considered reef-safe, though specimens have been reported to occasionally nip at Xenia and other soft corals if underfed. As with other Hemitaurichthys species, it is believed to form pairs during spawning and aquarists tend to keep it singly, as bonded pairs, or in small groups of 3 or more rather than just two unrelated individuals. Like many reef fish kept long-term, it has been reported to be susceptible to Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE) when diet or water quality is poor, so a varied diet with marine algae and vitamin-enriched foods is generally advised.
breedingNotes: Reported as oviparous with pair-forming spawning behaviour in the wild (FishBase). Captive breeding in home aquaria is not commonly documented.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source; gave family, peaceful/reef-safe rating, 'easy' care level, water parameters (72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12), 125 US gal minimum tank size, max size 7 inches, and feeding/temperament guidance.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), authoritative Indian Ocean native range (East Africa and Oman to Andaman Sea, western Sumatra and south-western Java), max length 18 cm TL, wild temperature 24.7-29°C, depth 1-40 m, planktivore/algae diet, and pair-forming oviparous spawning behaviour.

additional.name: Cross-source aquarium retailer summary (Marine Collectors, Violet Aquarium, Top Shelf Aquatics)
additional.notes: Confirmed reef-safe rating, ~125 US gal common minimum (some outlets list 75 US gal), peaceful temperament, shy on import, and occasional Xenia/soft-coral nipping when underfed. Salinity range 1.022-1.026 confirmed across multiple outlets, consistent with LiveAquaria's 1.020-1.025.
